message:
test(unistd): GaicbShape must mirror full glibc gaicb layout (bd-el0v8)

GaicbShape was 32 bytes (4 pointer fields) but glibc's struct gaicb
adds a trailing \`int __return; int __unused[5]\` (24 more bytes,
56 total). host_gai_error / host_gai_cancel / host_gai_suspend all
read the \`__return\` field — when called on our 32-byte stack-
allocated GaicbShape, glibc reads PAST the struct boundary into
adjacent stack memory.

Single-threaded the adjacent stack is consistently zero (compiler
zero-inits + first-use of frame). Under --test-threads=16 the
worker thread's stack is reused across tests, leaving residual
non-zero data above our 32-byte GaicbShape. host_gai_error then
returns whatever happens to be at offset 32, manifesting as
"left=0 right=2" or "left=0 right=121360248" assertion failures.

The earlier GAI_TEST_LOCK fix (31f5b9f2) didn't help because the
adjacent-stack contamination is set by NON-gai tests in the same
binary that just happen to run on the same worker thread before us.

Extend GaicbShape to the full 56-byte layout (\`__return\` + 5-element
\`__unused\`) and update the two struct-literal call sites accordingly.
Now host_gai_* reads our zero-initialized __return = 0 every time.

Verified single-pass: 14/14 gai tests pass under default thread count.
8-iteration --test-threads=16 stress in flight.
